front: Novel: Title character escapes/leaves Colonel Delmare (loveless marriage)
Novel: Sir Ralph (cousin) — Indiana and Ralph attempt suicide by jumping into a waterfall together
Novel: Set on the island of Reunion (French colony)
Novel: Noun — Creole maid seduced by / drowns herself after pregnancy by Raymon
Novel: Raymon de Ramiere — the rake / seducer
Novel: Jules Neraud provided travel diary / descriptions of Reunion (Sand had never been there)
Novel: Indiana implies the blackness / Creole identity of Noun (grew up together on Reunion)
Novel: July Revolution of 1830 — Indiana is caught in it after a letter from Raymon brings her to Paris
Novel: Title character is a Spanish orphan / zingarella modeled on Pauline Viardot
Novel: Maestro Porpora discovers / teaches Consuelo singing
Novel: Consuelo is an opera singer (soprano)
Novel: Castle of the Giants / Rudolstadt family / sent to Bohemia
Novel: Consuelo rejects Count Zustiniani
Novel: Set in Venice
Novel: First published in Revue Indépendante (periodical Sand co-founded)
Novel: Consuelo meets Haydn while fleeing to Vienna
Novel: Sequel The Countess of Rudolstadt directly influenced Walt Whitman
Novel: Character claims to have tried to steal business secrets after falling from a wall while meeting a maid
Novel: Autobiographical travel novel / travelogue about stay with Chopin
Novel: Tubercular / consumptive lover (Chopin) during the stay
Novel: Stay at Valldemossa / Carthusian monastery
Novel: Chopin composing music during the stay
Novel: Describes 'incredible slowness' of agriculture at the title location
Novel: Robert Graves translated it; appended Jose Quadrado's 'Refutation' calling Sand 'most immoral of writers' and 'most obscene of women'
Novel: Fadette makes Landry dance with her in exchange for helping him find his sickly twin Sylvinet
Novel: Sylvinet — sickly twin brother of Landry
Novel: Fadette cures Sylvinet at the end
Novel: Sylvinet joins the army after falling in love with his twin brother's wife
Novel: A widower travels to a farm to find a new wife but falls for a shepherdess traveling with him
back: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable) (George Sand)
work: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable)

front: Novel: Inspired the painting Ploughing in the Nivernais
back: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable) (George Sand)
work: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable)

front: Novel: First appendix describes supernatural stories told by the village hemp-dresser on autumn nights
back: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable) (George Sand)
work: The Devil's Pool (La Mare au Diable)

front: Novel: Bernard de Mauprat helps cousin Edmee escape savage relatives, extracts marriage promise by threatening rape
Novel: Edmee civilizes Bernard based on Rousseau's Emile
Author: Lover / partner of Frederic Chopin
Author: Wrote under a male pseudonym / cross-dressing French author
Author: Four pastoral novels appear in early scene of Proust's Swann's Way (gifted to narrator by mother/grandmother)
Author: Alfred de Musset affair — fictionalized as Brigitte in Confession of a Child of the Century; She and He controversy
Author: Gustave Flaubert correspondence — Flaubert addressed Sand as 'Master'
Author: Elizabeth Barrett Browning wrote poems to Sand: 'A Desire' and 'A Recognition'
Author: called her 'large-brained woman and large-hearted man'
Author: George Eliot defended Sand in a letter about morality vs. artistry
Author: Goncourt brothers describe Sand smoking cigars 'like an automaton', working only 1-4 AM
Author: Celia Madden in The Damnation of Theron Ware likens herself to Sand
Author: Hachette's founder Louis Hachette sold Sand's books
Author: Also had affairs with Jules Sandeau and Prosper Merimee
Author: Depicted in Iwaszkiewicz's play A Summer in Nohant alongside her kids and lover
